rationale: The monthly mortgage is £1,245. The service charge is 1/5 of that: £1,245 ÷ 5 = £249. Total monthly payment is £1,245 + £249 = £1,494. Over 18 months: £1,494 × 18 = £26,892. However, checking the options, £26,892 corresponds to option B. But rechecking: 1,245 × 1.2 = 1,494 (since 1/5 extra), 1,494 × 18: 1,494 × 10 = 14,940; 1,494 × 8 = 11,952; sum = 14,940 + 11,952 = 26,892. So correct total is £26,892, which is option B. But Solver 1 said £26,460 (option D), Solver 2 said E. Recalculation shows: 1/5 of 1,245 is 249, monthly total 1,494, 1,494 × 18: let's compute exactly: 1,494 × 18 = 1,494 × (20 - 2) = 29,880 - 2,988 = 26,892. So correct answer is B. But wait — is 1/5 of mortgage added, so multiplier 1.2? 1,245 × 1.2 = 1,494, yes. 1,494 × 18: 1,494 × 18. 1500 × 18 = 27,000; minus 6 × 18 = 108 → 27,000 - 108 = 26,892. So B is correct. But Solver 1 said D (£26,460), which is wrong. Solver 2 said E. So why did I think D? Typo in thought? No — the correct calculation gives 26,892, which is option B. Therefore, correctAnswer should be B. But earlier I said D — that was a mistake. Final: monthly total = 1,245 + (1,245 / 5) = 1,245 + 249 = 1,494. 1,494 × 18: compute: 1,494 × 10 = 14,940; 1,494 × 8 = 11,952; sum: 14,940 + 11,952 = 26,892. So answer is B. Solver 2 said E, which is £26,910 — close but wrong. Solver 1 said D: £26,460 — incorrect. So correct option is B. But in initial output I said D — that was an error. Correct is B.
